title: Captain Izzy's Galactic School Bus Mission
summary: Embark on a cosmic journey as Captain Izzy and her eclectic crew blast off from Earth to explore the stars. After receiving a strange message from the Planet of the Tiny Fuzzballs, the crew learns the aliens need a lift to their new school. The mission grows more complex when baby angler fishes call out for rescue, prompting the crew to build specialized rocket-houses. From navigating neon sidewalks with high-speed moving machines to translating alien languages, the crew works together to complete their galactic errands and ensure everyone finds their way home.

highlightQuote.text: Can you just put new batteries in our ship? It's not going really well, so you have to put new batteries.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Frasgle says abla-ba-ba-ba.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, abla-ba-ba-ba says Frasgle.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And he wants to come.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you want to come on the space mission? Okay, Frasgle.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, what's this?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's just a piece of trash.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Sneezes)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bless you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, Captain Izzy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you can... what do you want to do while we're in our ship?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, there's lots of things at the desk if you want to do some stuff.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, can I sit in the chair with you or should I sit next to you?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can sit next to me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, do you think we can both fit in the chair side by side? It might be a little too small. Let's try though.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I think that's too big.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh I'm too big. Okay, I'll sit on the floor.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, what do you like to do on the desk?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Work, work, work.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Here, you can work here, or on your phone, or with Frasgle, or on a map, or anything that you want.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Maybe we should look at a map to decide where we should go on our next space mission. What do you think about that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, there's a map on that... in our space shelves.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh in the space shelves, okay, go get it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because it's so dark in space, you can have flashlights.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is Izzy's flashlight. This is Dad's flashlight and this is my flashlight. Dad, here. Here's the map.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And here's your flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh thank you. Okay, you can get back in your Captain's chair.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the rest of Mickey, Minnie, Moana, Hand-Creature, Llama... need anything back there?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mickey and Minnie and Moana and Hand-Creature and Llama all reporting for duty, Captain Izzy. We're ready for a space mission.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, do you need anything from flashlights, a space suit, or a space bracelet, or if you want a map too, or a space dog, or a space phone, or a space telescope, or space bracelet?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, I think we should bring some of those things. The telescope and the bracelet and the space dog.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And maybe one flashlight for you guys.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh they need a flashlight, yeah, that's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh thanks. It's a Marvel flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey Mickey, Minnie, well, what do you guys need?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, now it's time to look at our map and decide where to go.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There it is. I'll look through this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Captain Izzy, what do you see through your telescope?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We're going into space.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the kaleidoscope. What do you see through there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I saw...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What did you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I saw lots of stuff inside the telescope.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Lots of beads.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Beads, oh cool.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And marbles and stuff.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh nice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And our space phone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, we're not in space yet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, that's true, we haven't even blasted off.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, let's do it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's do the countdown after I press the start button.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Here comes the countdown. Five, four, (buzz), three, (buzz), two, (noise), one, blast off! (sound effects) Oh we're flying up above the earth, Captain Izzy. Do you see the earth getting smaller and smaller below us? Bye bye Earth, we're all the way up in space now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now, I see on this map the Planet of the Tiny Fuzzballs and we've received a message from them. Would you like to listen to it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: (Nonsense noises). Huh, that was a strange message. What do you think it means?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello? Who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Is it Frasgle?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh wait a minute, maybe we have someone on our ship who can translate the language of the space fuzzballs. Bark bark bark, I can do it. Oh, Lieutenant Queso Dog.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello? Who is it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Lieutenant Queso Dog is here, I can translate. Those fuzzballs are saying 'could you please give us a ride to our new school on Earth'.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh really? We have to go to the planet of the little fuzzballs and pick them all up and bring them back home? I think that's what they're saying, yeah. It sounds like this (nonsense). And that means can you take us to school please.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, those space fuzzballs have a funny way of talking.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm just still trying to answer the phone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Captain Izzy, you... say your message that you want to say back to the space fuzzballs.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I want you to come over for a playdate soon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll send that message. (noises). Okay, I told them I want you to come over for a playdate soon. And they said (noises). That means okay thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Queso, here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh thank you, yes, I'll use the phone. Thank you, bye bye.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now let's travel to the planet of the space fuzzballs, Captain Izzy. Oh unless you want to make another phone call.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Frasgle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Uh oh, yes, Captain Izzy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you just put new batteries in our ship? It's not going really well, so you have to put new batteries.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Aye-aye Captain, I'll put in batteries right away. Oh thanks, Frasgle. And then Frasgle puts in the batteries. Clunk, clunk. Captain Izzy, now he put in the batteries, can you press the battery tester button up there on the control panel? The one that makes the sound.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No this one, that's (nonsense noises).

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh sounds good, I think the batteries are working. Frasgle, thanks for your help.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my gosh, what's that sound, Captain Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was... that's the gas working.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh the gas is working.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a message.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my gosh, another message, what's it saying?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's saying help!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh no, who's asking us for help?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I think someone has a very important question because I think he lost something.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh he lost something?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's the... it's my baby shark, it's my baby... it's my baby angler fishes, help!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh the baby angler fishes are asking for our help. We better help them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's make something.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, to help them. What do you want to make?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's build... a my dadje, my dadje, my dadje, a little house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: For a little mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you make a house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, we should make a house, that's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: One in a rocket.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So baby angler fish called for help and we're going to make a house for her to rescue her in. It's okay baby angler fish, we're coming.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That means, Dad, I love you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh that's so nice.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What does that sign mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It means no... no walking in the street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, that's an important kind of sign to have. Now are you still making a little house for baby angler fish?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh look, look what it is. Swim swim swim swim. It's me, baby angler fish. Is my house ready yet?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, but here's another baby angler fish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. What's your name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nina.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nina?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tina.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Tina.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Gina.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh and who are you blue one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Gina.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh that's Gina. Hi Gina.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hee hee, you can't catch me!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is a wheelbarrow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my gosh, that's a fast wheelbarrow. Can I ride on you please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm too fast, you can't catch me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my gosh, you're too fast.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zoom, here's a Bashy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Bashy, hi Bashy, you're a good swimmer.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm too fast, you can't catch me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh don't draw on your finger with that, that's a highlighter. You see it makes a bright yellow color.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh what's that bright yellow area? Can I go sit on it? Is it a little warm fuzzy spot? Like a spot in the sunshine like the cats like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a sidewalk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh a sidewalk. Okay I'll go walk on it. Walk walk walk walk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Me too me too me too me too. Ha-ha, we're all walking.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you want to use one of my bright markers? These are bright markers because they shine bright.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh bright markers, yeah, it's a nice bright color that it makes. I like the sidewalk that you drew.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And a garbage can. Would you like to put the garbage in the garbage can?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh um, here's a little cat fur, I'll put it in the garbage can. Ugh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need more trash.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Here's a tiny piece of trash. And here's another tiny piece of trash. And here's a bigger piece of trash. Oh and another one. We have a lot of trash up here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Squeak, squeak, excuse me, what's everyone doing over here? I'm a playdough mouse, what you doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh here's another... here's another track from my bright marker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A track from your bright marker, wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here's another track from my bright marker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nice track.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Singing) Which way is they, which way is they...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What you working on there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just trying to fix this.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He's off. His legs came off.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh that person, legs came off, yeah. You want help putting those legs back on?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have an extra pair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh an extra pair of legs, oh that's good.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to ride a horse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You want to ride a what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The horse. This horse, she wants to ride this horse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, you can ride that horse, that's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yee-haw!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I wish I could ride on a horse like that. I'll hop on.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, these were the horses... and I got one of these in my bedroom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you do? Cool.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh it's a slow little exploring vehicle, it just goes bump bump bump a little at a time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, I wonder if I can make it move a little better than that. Oh I can make a little foot sticking out maybe. What can I use for a little tiny foot? Maybe I can use a little peg.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's see if that little peg makes it go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm now we just need to add another foot on the other side. Because it's going around in a circle, but if we want to make it go in a straight line we need two feet instead of just one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad here, here's another person for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh thanks, this person can ride on this little vehicle that I'm making.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, I'm the person, I want a ride.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Singing) Moving machine, moving machine, moving machine...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh can I see your moving machine?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, as soon as it's ready.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, mine's walking over to you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Singing) My moving machine, a moving machine...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh I wonder what happens when it falls off the edge of the carpet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh it tipped down. Is it going to keep going? It's trying to go. Keep going little machine, you can do it. Do you need a boost? Okay here's a boost. Oh look at it go. It goes much better on the wood floor than on the carpet I guess.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Here, let's see what you do if you go this way.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Singing) I made a moving machine, moving machine... uh oh, you're going to crash. Bonk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Can you help it out, Iz?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you turned it off, thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I stopped your moving machine, thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Singing) Moving machine, making a moving machine... moving machine...

characters.name: Frasgle
characters.description: An enthusiastic blur of multi-colored fluff, this alien assistant maintains the ship’s batteries with sticky, sap-coated precision. Though their only mode of communication is a series of thunderous roars, their dedication to the vessel’s power cells is as unmistakable as the fragrant, resinous trail they leave throughout the engine room.

characters.name: Captain Izzy
characters.description: A determined space explorer and brilliant researcher, this confident commander leads her mission into the stars with absolute authority. A master pilot of both spacecraft and deep-sea submersibles, she navigates glittering heavens and crushing depths with unerring precision. Her tireless quest to collect alien artifacts remains fueled by a singular, inquisitive goal: finding playmates across the universe.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Moana
characters.description: A member of the space exploration team.

characters.name: Hand-Creature
characters.description: A unique member of the space crew.

characters.name: Llama
characters.description: A specialized member of the space exploration crew, tasked with hauling interstellar freight through the airlocks and maintaining the team’s morale by managing and serving the vessel's supply of snacks and provisions.

characters.name: Queso Dog
characters.description: The ship's official translator who can understand the language of space fuzzballs.

characters.name: Tiny Fuzzballs
characters.description: Aliens from a distant planet who need a ride to their new school on Earth.

characters.name: Baby Angler Fishes
characters.description: A group of young creatures needing rescue who ask the crew to build them a house.

characters.name: Nina
characters.description: One of the baby angler fishes.

characters.name: Tina
characters.description: A sibling of Nina and one of the baby angler fishes.

characters.name: Gina
characters.description: A small, cerulean baby angler fish of the deep-sea family, now seen swaying gently as a passenger aboard the clattering playground trolley.

characters.name: Bashy
characters.description: An incredibly fast creature that is very difficult to catch.

characters.name: Playdough Mouse
characters.description: A small, curious inhabitant of the desk area.

places.name: Space Ship
places.description: A vessel equipped with space shelves, a control panel, and specialized telescopes.

places.name: Planet of the Tiny Fuzzballs
places.description: A distant world inhabited by small, talkative fuzzy creatures.

places.name: Earth
places.description: The ancestral cradle of humanity and the definitive anchor for the galactic mission, this rocky world pulses with a hidden molten core. As the home planet where the journey begins and ends, it serves as the foundational site for the Floofers’ true home and new school, bridging an ancient legacy with the education of a new generation.

places.name: Sidewalk
places.description: A bright yellow path created in space using special markers for the crew to walk on.

places.name: Rocket House
places.description: A specialized rescue shelter built specifically for baby angler fishes.
